metadata.publisher: Federal Negarit Gazette (Ministry of Finance archive signed copy)
metadata.source_authority: Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia (House of Peoples' Representatives)
metadata.source_note: Ministry of Finance archive signed copy of the Income Tax (Amendment) Proclamation No. 1395/2025 - an advance print carrying the signature block but a blank gazette number on the title (the gazette-numbered Federal Negarit Gazette print is not publicly digitized), obtained via the metaappz Ethiopian-laws reference collection and byte-verified against the SOUTHMOD/ETMOD harness copy. The document is a 51-page bilingual (Amharic/English) scan whose embedded text layer is garbled, so extraction runs a fresh OCR at 350 dpi (eng, psm 3, force_ocr) over the whole file as a single block; the Amharic pages OCR to noise (harmless) while the English pages carry the operative text. Grounds: the Schedule A employment monthly bands 2,000 / 4,000 / 7,000 / 10,000 / 14,000 Birr at 15/20/25/30/35 percent (first 2,000 exempt); the Schedule C business annual bands 24,000 / 48,000 / 84,000 / 120,000 / 168,000; the presumptive/turnover schedule 100,000 / 500,000 / 1,000,000 / 1,500,000 / 2,000,000 at 2/3/5/7/9 percent; Article 23 Minimum Alternative Tax (tax below 2.5% of turnover, reduced by business income tax, not applying to a taxpayer under the total gross revenue tax regime in Article 49); the Category A / Category B split at Birr 2,000,000 gross revenue; and the effective date of 8 July 2025. Flag: MoF-archive signed advance copy, gazette-numbered print not publicly digitized, fresh 350 dpi OCR over a bilingual scan.
metadata.title: Income Tax (Amendment) Proclamation No. 1395/2025 - Schedule A/B/C and presumptive rate tables; Article 23 Minimum Alternative Tax; Category A/B threshold Birr 2,000,000; effective 8 July 2025

metadata.publisher: Federal Negarit Gazette (Berhanena Selam Printing Enterprise)
metadata.source_authority: Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia (House of Peoples' Representatives)
metadata.source_note: Genuine Federal Negarit Gazette Extraordinary Issue, 22nd Year No. 104, Addis Ababa 18 August 2016 (title-page issue line and date verified on the face), a faithful reproduction hosted in the metaappz Ethiopian- laws reference collection and byte-verified against the SOUTHMOD/ETMOD harness copy. Text-embedded and clean, extracted as a single block with no OCR. This is the base Schedule A/B/C proclamation the 2025 amendment operates on. Grounds: Article 10(3) "An employee shall not be allowed a deduction"; Article 27(1)(c) employer pension/provident contribution deductible only up to the part not "in excess of 15%" of monthly employment income; Article 65(1)(c) the same 15%-of-monthly-employment- income ceiling ("does not exceed 15% of the monthly employment income").
metadata.title: Federal Income Tax Proclamation No. 979/2016 - Article 10(3) employment deduction bar; Articles 27(1)(c) and 65(1)(c) 15%-of-monthly-employment-income limits

metadata.source_authority: Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia (House of Peoples' Representatives)
metadata.source_note: Genuine Federal Negarit Gazeta, 17th Year No. 79, Addis Ababa 24 June 2011 (issue line and running head verified on the face), a faithful reproduction hosted in the metaappz Ethiopian-laws reference collection and byte-verified against the SOUTHMOD/ETMOD harness copy. Text- embedded, extracted as a single block with no OCR. Grounds Article 10 of the contribution split, verbatim "1/ by the employer, 11%;" and "2/ by the employee, 7%." (This 2011 proclamation was later repealed by Proclamation No. 1268/2022; captured here for the CY-range the ETMOD pension parity validates.)
metadata.title: Private Organization Employees' Pension Proclamation No. 715/2011 - Article 10 contribution split (employer 11%, employee 7%)

metadata.source_authority: Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia (House of Peoples' Representatives)
metadata.source_note: Genuine Federal Negarit Gazeta, 17th Year No. 78, Addis Ababa 24 June 2011 (issue line and running head verified on the face), a faithful reproduction hosted in the metaappz Ethiopian-laws reference collection and byte-verified against the SOUTHMOD/ETMOD harness copy. Text- embedded, extracted as a single block with no OCR. Grounds Article 10 of the contribution split for public servants, verbatim "by the public office, 11%;" and "by the public servant, 7%."; and Article 11 for members of the defence force and the police, verbatim "1/ by the public office, 25%;" and "2/ by the public servant, 7%." (This 2011 proclamation was later repealed by Proclamation No. 1267/2022; captured here for the CY-range the ETMOD pension parity validates.)
metadata.title: Public Servants' Pension Proclamation No. 714/2011 - Article 10 split (11%/7%) and Article 11 military & police split (public office 25%)

metadata.source_authority: Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia (House of Peoples' Representatives)
metadata.source_note: Official Ministry of Finance copy of the Value Added Tax Proclamation No. 1341/2024, the Federal Negarit Gazette No. 61, Addis Ababa 21 August 2024 facsimile with annex, served from mofed.gov.et and byte-verified against the SOUTHMOD/ETMOD harness copy (this official MoF copy is preferred over the metaappz reproduction). Text-embedded, extracted as a single block with no OCR. Grounds Article 8 (Imposition of Value Added Tax), sub-article 2, the standard rate, verbatim "b) in any other case 15 percent." (zero percent applying to the enumerated exports/supplies). Repeals the Value Added Tax Proclamation No. 285/2002.
metadata.title: Value Added Tax Proclamation No. 1341/2024 - Article 8 imposition; standard rate "in any other case 15 percent"; repeals VAT Proc 285/2002
